description_long: This is a traditional djembe drum, featuring a goblet-shaped wooden body with a natural, slightly weathered finish. The drumhead is made of taut animal skin, secured by a series of black tension ropes running vertically down the sides. The base of the drum is adorned with carved geometric patterns, adding a decorative touch to its utilitarian form. The drum's surface shows some signs of use, with minor discoloration and wear on the drumhead and body. The overall construction appears sturdy, with the drumhead tightly stretched and the body solidly carved. This type of drum is commonly used in street performances, parks, and community gatherings.
description: A traditional djembe drum with a carved wooden body, taut animal skin drumhead, and black tension ropes. The surface shows minor wear and decorative carvings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the drumhead, black tension ropes, and carved geometric patterns on the base.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the tautness of the drumhead and the curvature of the goblet-shaped body.
description_view_2: Side view with a clearer look at the lighter wood finish and the full pattern around the base.
description_view_3: Rear view emphasizing the vertical tension ropes and the even stretch of the drumhead.
